title: Oliguria - Acute Kidney Injury
chief_complaint: Elderly patient post-colonoscopy prep with decreased urine output for 24 hours, confusion, and creatinine rise from 1.0 to 3.2 mg/dL.

physical_exam.general: Dry mucous membranes, decreased skin turgor, orthostatic hypotension

hidden_data.ua: Specific gravity: 1.030, No casts, No protein

patient_responses.bowel_prep: I drank the whole bottle of colonoscopy prep
patient_responses.dizziness: I feel dizzy when I stand

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Pre-renal AKI from hypovolemia.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is pre-renal AKI. The BUN/Cr >20, UNa <20, and FENa <1% confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Pre-renal AKI: IV fluids (NS bolus), monitor urine output, hold nephrotoxins. FENa <1% is key. Most cases reversible with timely fluid resuscitation. Good catch.
